title: ELC & Elementary Teacher
summary: Teacher role providing classroom instruction to deaf students from preschool through fifth grade. The position plans and modifies curriculum, prepares lessons and instructional materials aligned to student ability levels and IEPs, tracks student progress, and supports ARD/IEP processes.
required_credentials: Certification may include Deaf and Hard of Hearing, special education, or certification in the assigned subject and level, or ability to obtain valid Texas certification
Signed Language Proficiency Interview level of Advanced at hire, with supporting documentation submitted during application
preferred_credentials: Sign communication skill rating above the required level
Master's degree in special education, deafness, or a related field
preferred_experience: Prior experience in deaf education or a related field
responsibilities: Provide classroom instruction to deaf students from preschool through fifth grade.
Plan, develop, present, and modify curriculum.
Prepare lesson plans and instructional materials aligned to student ability levels and IEPs.
Evaluate and document student progress data.
Serve as an advisor or advocate during ARD processes.
Collaborate with students, parents, and staff to develop IEPs through ARD committees.
Implement instructional, therapeutic, or skill-development programs for assigned students.
Prepare written reports about student progress on IEP goals.
Teach subject matter according to IEP guidance.
Use instructional strategies, activities, materials, and equipment that reflect assigned students' learning styles and needs.
Work with the instructional team to modify curriculum as needed.
Submit weekly lesson plans aligned to the established curriculum.
Assess student learning styles and use results to plan instruction.
Use technology in the teaching and learning process.
Manage student behavior according to the code of conduct and individual behavior programs.
rubric_hints: Deaf education or special education background
demo_notes: Strong credential-verification demo option because it has multiple hard gates: Texas teaching certification plus advanced sign-language proficiency documentation.
